more.title: How the Skeleton of Mutapa Times is Based on Sarah Charlesworth's Work
more.description: Conceptual artist Sarah Charlesworth's 1979 study of how newspapers covered a solar eclipse inspired the framework behind The Mutapa Times.

more.title: Zim Fuel Prices Jump as Global Oil Tensions Push Costs Higher
more.description: Fuel prices in Zimbabwe have increased with immediate effect, the Zimbabwe Energy Regulatory Authority (ZERA) has announced citing rising global oil costs. In a notice issued late on Wednesday, ZERA said the revised prices came into force on 4 March 2026 and will remain valid [...]The post Zim Fuel Prices Jump as Global Oil Tensions Push Costs Higher appeared first on 263Chat.

more.title: Zimbabwe Has Three Months of Fuel Reserves as Global Oil Prices Push Pump Costs Up
more.description: Zimbabwe Government Assures Nation of Adequate Fuel Supplies Amid Global Tensions Government has moved to reassure the nation that Zimbabwe’s fuel supply remains secure, even as escalating tensions in the Middle East continue to push global oil prices upward. Authorities say the country has enough fuel to sustain national demand for the next three months, [...]The post Zimbabwe Has Three Months of Fuel Reserves as Global Oil Prices Push Pump Costs Up appeared first on iHarare News.

more.title: New ZiG Banknotes Gazetted Ahead Of 7 April Circulation
more.description: Finance, Economic Development and Investment Promotion Minister Mthuli Ncube has gazetted new ZiG banknotes that were unveiled last week by John Mushayavanhu, Governor of the Reserve Bank of Zimbabwe (RBZ), during the presentation of the 2026 Monetary Policy Statement.

more.title: Tobacco farmers must pursue quality to maintain prices

more.title: Diesel, petrol prices up
more.description: Source: Diesel, petrol prices up – herald Business Reporter THE Zimbabwe Energy Regulatory Authority has announced an increase in diesel and petrol blend prices, attributing this to shifts in the international market. With immediate effect, diesel will retail at US$1,77 per litre, while petrol blend (E5) will cost US$1,71 per litre.
